message:
fix(http): RFC 7230 TE+CL precedence and response splitting defense

body_kind(): When both Transfer-Encoding and Content-Length headers
are present, Content-Length is now ignored per RFC 7230 §3.3.3
(TE takes precedence). Previously returned BodyKind::Empty which
silently dropped the body. The private has_transfer_encoding()
helper is removed since is_chunked() already covers the check.

ResponseHead::serialize(): Sanitize the reason phrase by stripping
CR/LF bytes to prevent HTTP response splitting attacks. RFC 7230
defines reason-phrase as *(HTAB / SP / VCHAR / obs-text), which
excludes CR and LF.

Tests:
- body_kind_te_plus_cl_uses_chunked: verifies TE+CL resolves to
  Chunked instead of Empty
- response_head_serialize_strips_crlf_from_reason: verifies CRLF
  injection in reason phrase is neutralized

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
